Ice_Log: 1700: At 5:00 p.m. received radiogram from Nome informing vessels bound to Nome that there were no openings in the ice. leading to shore from Port Safety to Sledge Island, and that vessels wuld not be able to reach Nome until fresh Northerly winds . set in. Vessel standing along edge of ice pack, looking for a lead in the ice to shore.

0200 - 0400: Passed through detached pieces of ice.
1035: From 1035 to end of watch steamed along edge of ice pack.
Noon to 4PM: Passed through several loose ice fields.

Ice_Log: 2320: Steaming along edge of ice pack.
0200: Entered pack ice. From 2:00 to 3:00 working through pack ice to Eastward of False Cape in a NE direction.

0300: 3:00 to 4:00 working out of solid pack ice in a SW direction at various speeds and courses, unabe to proceed owing to pack. being held on shore by Westerly winds which have prevailed generally throughout the season, young ice forming and. cementing floes.
4AM to 8AM: Heavy field ice throughout the watch.
0645: Anchored on account of fog and ice.

Note: 0720: Underway, course made good SSW, dist.14 miles for the watch.
0810: Anchored on account of fog. 8AM: Point Franklin bearing E1/2N (mag) distant 7.0 miles.
